content:
Authentication proves who you are. Authorization decides what you are allowed to do. Knowing the difference helps you understand how every secure system works.

5 min read
Just now

--

Non-members can read here
Almost every website and application asks you to sign in before you can use it.
You enter your username and password. Sometimes you also enter a one-time code from your phone. After that, you can access your account, files, or settings.
Many beginners think this whole process is called authentication.
It is not.
There are actually two different security processes working together.
The first process checks your identity.
The second process decides what you are allowed to access.
These two ideas are called authentication and authorization.
People often use these terms as if they mean the same thing. They do not. Understanding the difference is one of the first steps in learning cybersecurity, Linux administration, cloud computing, and DevOps.
In this article, you will learn what authentication and authorization are, how they work, why both are important, and how they work together in real life.
